next_steps: Endobronchial ultrasound-guided transbronchial needle aspiration (EBUS-TBNA) of mediastinal/hilar lymph nodes
Serum ACE level and 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D levels
18F-FDG PET/CT to identify optimal biopsy sites and assess for cardiac involvement
Serologic testing for Bartonella henselae (IgG and IgM) and QuantiFERON-TB Gold
Endobronchial ultrasound-guided transbronchial needle aspiration (EBUS-TBNA) of mediastinal lymph nodes for histology, flow cytometry, and microbial culture
Cardiac MRI with gadolinium enhancement to evaluate for myocardial inflammation or infiltration
Continuous cardiac rhythm monitoring (Holter or telemetry) to assess for conduction disturbances or arrhythmias
Skin punch biopsy of lower extremity lesions to assess for sarcoid granulomas or vasculitis
Serum ACE levels, 1,25-dihydroxyvitamin D, and inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP)
Full autoimmune serology panel (ANA, ANCA, RF, anti-CCP)
18F-FDG PET/CT for assessment of systemic disease distribution and selection of biopsy sites
